interactions.final_answer:
## Phase 0 — Complete design proposal (for approval) ### 1) SCOPE CONFIG (JPMorgan Chase corporate intranet hub) **Scope name:** JPMorgan Chase — Internal Communications **Description:**

Company-wide, fact-checked updates and guidance for a G-SIB operating at the center of global finance—written with earned confidence and reviewed for accuracy.

**Welcome message:**

Welcome to JPMorgan Chase Internal Communications. Here you’ll find reviewed updates on performance, risk, technology, people priorities, and mandatory policies—so you can act with clarity and confidence.

**image_url:** jp/logo.png **Brand colors (from profile):** - **primary_color:** #003B7A - **primary_color_contrast:** #FFFFFF - **secondary_color:** #117ACA - **warn_color:** #C8102E - **warn_color_contrast:** #FFFFFF **Highlights:** - **number_of_highlights:** 4 - **highlights_carousel:** true - **highlights_style:** BannerCarousel **Footer (3 columns; real URLs from profile):** 1. **About** → - Business Principles: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/about/governance/business-principles - Annual Report: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/annual-report 2. **Investors** → - Investor Relations: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/ir - ESG / Sustainability: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/impact 3. **Get Help** → - Careers: https://careers.jpmorgan.com - Newsroom: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/news-stories **Social links (from profile):** - LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/company/jpmorgan-chase - Twitter/X: https://twitter.com/jpmorgan - YouTube: https://youtube.com/jpmorgan **navigation_links:** - **topBarLinks (3):** - Newsroom: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/news-stories - Investor Relations: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/ir - Careers: https://careers.jpmorgan.com - **footerLinks (5 total):** - Business Principles: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/about/governance/business-principles - Annual Report: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/annual-report - ESG / Sustainability: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/impact - J.P. Morgan (institutional): https://www.jpmorgan.com - Onyx: https://www.jpmorgan.com/onyx - **socialLinks:** LinkedIn, Twitter/X, YouTube (as above) **sidebar_components (2 selected):** - upcomingEvents - recentDocuments --- ### 2) CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5 channels; 3 PRIMARY pinned) #### Ch1 (PRIMARY) — “Fortress Balance Sheet & Earnings” - **Purpose:** Monthly/quarterly performance updates with risk-aware context (capital ratios, earnings drivers, and what it means operationally). - **Type:** public - **Color:** #003B7A - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** jp/financial_report.png - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description (HTML):**

Reviewed earnings and performance updates—written in terms we use internally: net revenue, net income, and the capital and risk context behind the numbers.

Where relevant, we connect segment performance to the operating environment and to risk management considerations.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** Articles - **default_role:** Reader - **FAQ (3):** - Q: “Where do I find the quarterly numbers JPMorgan is communicating externally?” A: “In this channel—articles summarize FY2024 and quarterly items and link to the company’s investor materials. For regulatory or detailed filings, use Investor Relations.” - Q: “When should I expect capital updates like CET1 discussion?” A: “When the firm publishes quarterly reporting—typically around earnings and investor communications; updates here reflect the timing and the reviewed summary.” - Q: “Do these posts replace my team’s risk review process?” A: “No. This hub is factual context; operational decisions still follow your line-of-business risk governance.” #### Ch2 (PRIMARY) — “Markets & Client Coverage” - **Purpose:** CIB-focused updates on markets performance, league table context, and client-coverage rhythms (clients, not speculation). - **Type:** public - **Color:** #003B7A - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** jp/stock_market.png - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description (HTML):**

This channel captures reviewed updates across markets—fixed income trading, equities, FX, and the execution environment that affects client outcomes.

It also reinforces how our client coverage model turns relationships into mandates, execution, and league-table outcomes.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** Articles - **default_role:** Reader - **FAQ (3):** - Q: “What does JPMorgan mean by ‘clients’ vs ‘customers’ internally?” A: “This channel uses CIB language (‘clients’) aligned to coverage and relationship management; the terminology signals the commercial model.” - Q: “How do league tables show up in internal communications?” A: “When rankings are materially updated—articles explain the internal communications context and where to find supporting external materials.” - Q: “Where can I find guidance on sharing deal-related information?” A: “If it involves MNPI, follow the Information Barrier policy and route questions to Legal/Compliance before communication.” #### Ch3 (PRIMARY) — “Technology, AI & Payments at Scale” - **Purpose:** Practical internal updates on technology initiatives—COIN, Athena, Fusion Data, LLM Suite, Onyx, and payments infrastructure. - **Type:** public - **Color:** #117ACA - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** jp/blockchain.png - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description (HTML):**

How we build and deploy: COIN, Athena, Fusion Data, the LLM Suite rollout, Onyx by J.P. Morgan, and payments infrastructure that supports real-time settlement.

Posts focus on what changed, why it matters, and what teams should do next—grounded in our reviewed technology program context.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** Articles - **default_role:** Reader - **FAQ (3):** - Q: “What is the LLM Suite used for at work?” A: “In-scope use cases include document summarisation, research generation, and code assistance—consistent with internal guidance.” - Q: “Is Onyx public-facing?” A: “This channel covers internal program context. For external announcements, use the company’s published materials and official links.” - Q: “Where do I go for security and access questions?” A: “Start with the IT & Security channel policies and consult the relevant security/compliance contacts for your area.” --- #### Ch4 (SECONDARY) — “People, Apprenticeships & Workforce Initiatives” - **Purpose:** Culture and workforce programs—apprenticeship model, return-to-office guidance (factual), and workforce development initiatives. - **Type:** public - **Color:** #4A4F55 - **is_sticky:** False - **image_url:** jp/community.png - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** True - **Description (HTML):**

Updates for how we hire, develop, and build capability—especially at junior levels through apprenticeship and multi-year programs.

We also communicate people-program milestones and workforce initiatives that support communities and talent pipelines.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** Articles - **default_role:** Reader - **FAQ:** - Q: “How does the Analyst → Associate → VP → Executive Director → Managing Director progression work?” A: “It’s the promotion track described for investment banking talent development; internal articles explain milestones and program context.” - Q: “Where can I find details about workforce programs like New Skills at Work?” A: “In this channel—each milestone article explains the initiative and what teams can expect next.” - Q: “Where do I find return-to-office policy guidance by role?” A: “Look for RTO policy updates here when guidance changes, and confirm details with your local HR contacts.” #### Ch5 (SECONDARY) — “Regulatory & Legal Guidance (Chinese Walls)” - **Purpose:** Mandatory, reviewed compliance guidance—especially Information Barrier/Chinese Walls—so employees can act without creating regulatory risk. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** #C8102E - **is_sticky:** False - **image_url:** jp/law.png - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** True - **hide_highlights:** True - **Description (HTML):**

This channel contains reviewed regulatory and legal guidance, including mandatory acknowledgements where required.

It is not a substitute for legal advice—if you have questions, consult Legal/Compliance before acting or communicating.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** Articles - **default_role:** None - **FAQ (3):** - Q: “What are Information Barriers (Chinese Walls)?” A: “They are mandatory restrictions between business units that may possess MNPI and those that may not, to prevent improper information sharing.” - Q: “Do these rules apply globally?” A: “Yes—there are no exceptions for seniority, relationship, or urgency.” - Q: “What should I do if I’m unsure whether a communication is permissible?” A: “Direct the question to Legal or Compliance before communicating.” --- ## 3) CONTENT CALENDAR (seeded; Published; real flags) ### PRIMARY channels: 3 seeded articles each #### Ch1 — Fortress Balance Sheet & Earnings (3) 1. **Title:** FY2024 Earnings Summary: ~$166B Net Revenue, ~$58B Net Income—What Drove the Record - **Type:** recap - **Tags:** ['fy2024', 'earnings', 'fortress-balance-sheet', 'capital', 'segments'] - **is_main_highlight:** True (1 of 4) - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** jp/financial_report.png 2. **Title:** Capital & Stress Test Season: How JPMorgan Prepares for DFAST/CCAR and What It Means Internally - **Type:** guide - **Tags:** ['regulatory-capital', 'stress-testing', 'risk-management', 'cete1', 'g-sib'] - **is_main_highlight:** True (2 of 4) - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** jp/risk.png 3. **Title:** Segment Pulse: FY2024 Revenue by Franchise—CIB ~$59B, CCB ~$60B, CB ~$15B, AWM ~$22B - **Type:** spotlight - **Tags:** ['segments', 'cib', 'ccb', 'awm', 'commercial-banking'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** jp/financial_report.png --- #### Ch2 — Markets & Client Coverage (3) 1. **Title:** Markets Review: Fixed Income, FX, Equities—How We Translate VaR and Risk Measures into Client Confidence - **Type:** recap - **Tags:** ['markets', 'vaR', 'liquidity-risk', 'fixed-income', 'credit-risk'] - **is_main_highlight:** True (3 of 4) - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** jp/stock_market.png 2. **Title:** League Tables Update: Why “#1 or #2” Matters Internally for M&A, ECM, DCM & Leveraged Loans - **Type:** milestone - **Tags:** ['league-tables', 'm&a', 'ecm', 'dcm', 'leveraged-loans'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** jp/stock_market.png 3. **Title:** Client Coverage Model in Practice: From Pitch Materials to Mandate and Tombstone Credit - **Type:** guide - **Tags:** ['client-coverage', 'cib', 'mandates', 'execution', 'fees'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** jp/agreement.png --- #### Ch3 — Technology, AI & Payments at Scale (3) 1. **Title:** LLM Suite (2024) Operating Guidance: Summarisation, Research, and Code Assistance with Reviewable Outputs - **Type:** guide - **Tags:** ['ai', 'llm-suite', 'productivity', 'risk', 'technology'] - **is_main_highlight:** True (4 of 4) - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** jp/blockchain.png 2. **Title:** Payments at Scale: Processing $10T+ Per Day and What “Real-Time” Changes for Commercial Banking - **Type:** spotlight - **Tags:** ['payments', 'treasury-services', 'real-time', 'commercial-banking', 'infrastructure'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** jp/payment.png 3. **Title:** COIN + Athena Together: Faster Document Review and Real-Time Pricing for Derivatives Execution - **Type:** recap - **Tags:** ['coin', 'athena', 'risk', 'derivatives', 'technology'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** jp/document_review.png --- ### SECONDARY channels: 2 seeded articles each #### Ch4 — People, Apprenticeships & Workforce Initiatives (2) 1. **Title:** Apprenticeship Model: Analyst & Associate Programs—Learning by Doing Through Live Transactions - **Type:** guide - **Tags:** ['apprenticeship', 'analyst-program', 'associate-program', 'people-development'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** jp/community.png 2. **Title:** Workforce Programs Milestone: New Skills at Work ($350M), Advancing Black Pathways, Veterans & ReEntry - **Type:** milestone - **Tags:** ['workforce-development', 'dei', 'veterans', 'reentry', 'impact'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** jp/community.png --- #### Ch5 — Regulatory & Legal Guidance (Chinese Walls) (2) 1. **Title:** Mandatory Acknowledgement: Information Barrier (Chinese Walls) Rules for MNPI Communications—No Exceptions - **Type:** policy - **Tags:** ['mnpi', 'information-barriers', 'chinese-walls', 'compliance', 'mandatory-acknowledgement'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** True - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** jp/law.png 2. **Title:** Regulatory Capital & Legal Operations Checklist: Stress Testing (DFAST/CCAR) Readiness and Escalation Paths - **Type:** policy - **Tags:** ['stress-testing', 'occ', 'federal-reserve', 'cce1', 'legal-compliance'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** True - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** jp/document_review.png > **Notes on “no placeholders / facts”:** all seeded article plans above are grounded in the provided profile figures and named programs/regulatory concepts. (During build, each article body will be authored in valid HTML with 6–8+ substantive paragraphs, using only profile facts.) --- ## 4) HUB-LEVEL POLISH (what ties it together) **company_name:** JPMorgan Chase & Co. **website_name:** JPMorgan Chase (institutional umbrella) (site baseline from profile URLs) **default_language:** en-US **supported_languages:** ['en-US'] (profile does not provide additional language operating targets) **login_quick_links (5 real URLs from profile):** - Investor Relations: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/ir - Newsroom: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/news-stories - Careers: https://careers.jpmorgan.com - ESG / Sustainability: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/impact - Business Principles: https://www.jpmorganchase.com/about/governance/business-principles **sidebar_navigation (ordered by priorities):** - collaborators - documents - approvalRequests - workgroups - learning - ideation --- # CONFIRMATION BLOCK — Phase 1 will build this end-to-end If you approve, I will: 1) Create the **scope** with the exact scope configuration above (including colors, footer, navigation, sidebar widgets, highlights settings). 2) Create **5 channels** with the exact architecture above (including sticky settings and default roles). 3) Seed articles in each channel with **Published** status and flags exactly as planned: - **3 primary channels × 3 articles each** - **2 secondary channels × 2 articles each** - **Exactly 4** articles will have **is_main_highlight=True** - **All policy/security/compliance** articles will have **require_read_confirmation=True** (Chinese Walls + regulatory checklist) Shall I proceed, or would you like to change anything?

arguments.name: JPMorgan Chase — Internal Communications
arguments.description: Company-wide, fact-checked updates and guidance for a G-SIB operating at the center of global finance—written with earned confidence and reviewed for accuracy.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome to JPMorgan Chase Internal Communications. Here you’ll find reviewed updates on performance, risk, technology, people priorities, and mandatory policies—so you can act with clarity and confidence.

channels.name: Fortress Balance Sheet & Earnings
channels.description:
Reviewed earnings and performance updates—written in terms we use internally: net revenue, net income, and the capital and risk context behind the numbers.

Where relevant, we connect segment performance to the operating environment and to risk management considerations.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I find the quarterly numbers JPMorgan is communicating externally?
frequent_questions.answer: In this channel—articles summarize FY2024 and quarterly items and link to the company’s investor materials. For regulatory or detailed filings, use Investor Relations.

frequent_questions.question: When should I expect capital updates like CET1 discussion?
frequent_questions.answer: When the firm publishes quarterly reporting—typically around earnings and investor communications; updates here reflect the timing and the reviewed summary.

frequent_questions.question: Do these posts replace my team’s risk review process?
frequent_questions.answer: No. This hub is factual context; operational decisions still follow your line-of-business risk governance.

channels.name: Markets & Client Coverage
channels.description:
This channel captures reviewed updates across markets—fixed income trading, equities, FX, and the execution environment that affects client outcomes.

It also reinforces how our client coverage model turns relationships into mandates, execution, and league-table outcomes.

frequent_questions.question: What does JPMorgan mean by ‘clients’ vs ‘customers’ internally?
frequent_questions.answer: This channel uses CIB language (‘clients’) aligned to coverage and relationship management; the terminology signals the commercial model.

frequent_questions.question: How do league tables show up in internal communications?
frequent_questions.answer: When rankings are materially updated—articles explain the internal communications context and where to find supporting external materials.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find guidance on sharing deal-related information?
frequent_questions.answer: If it involves MNPI, follow the Information Barrier policy and route questions to Legal/Compliance before communication.

channels.name: Technology, AI & Payments at Scale
channels.description:
How we build and deploy: COIN, Athena, Fusion Data, the LLM Suite rollout, Onyx by J.P. Morgan, and payments infrastructure that supports real-time settlement.

Posts focus on what changed, why it matters, and what teams should do next—grounded in our reviewed technology program context.

frequent_questions.question: What is the LLM Suite used for at work?
frequent_questions.answer: In-scope use cases include document summarisation, research generation, and code assistance—consistent with internal guidance.

frequent_questions.question: Is Onyx public-facing?
frequent_questions.answer: This channel covers internal program context. For external announcements, use the company’s published materials and official links.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I go for security and access questions?
frequent_questions.answer: Start with the IT & Security channel policies and consult the relevant security/compliance contacts for your area.

channels.name: People, Apprenticeships & Workforce Initiatives
channels.description:
Updates for how we hire, develop, and build capability—especially at junior levels through apprenticeship and multi-year programs.

We also communicate people-program milestones and workforce initiatives that support communities and talent pipelines.

frequent_questions.question: How does the Analyst → Associate → VP → Executive Director → Managing Director progression work?
frequent_questions.answer: It’s the promotion track described for investment banking talent development; internal articles explain milestones and program context.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find details about workforce programs like New Skills at Work?
frequent_questions.answer: In this channel—each milestone article explains the initiative and what teams can expect next.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I find return-to-office policy guidance by role?
frequent_questions.answer: Look for RTO policy updates here when guidance changes, and confirm details with your local HR contacts.

channels.name: Regulatory & Legal Guidance (Chinese Walls)
channels.description:
This channel contains reviewed regulatory and legal guidance, including mandatory acknowledgements where required.

It is not a substitute for legal advice—if you have questions, consult Legal/Compliance before acting or communicating.

frequent_questions.question: What are Information Barriers (Chinese Walls)?
frequent_questions.answer: They are mandatory restrictions between business units that may possess MNPI and those that may not, to prevent improper information sharing.

frequent_questions.question: Do these rules apply globally?
frequent_questions.answer: Yes—there are no exceptions for seniority, relationship, or urgency.

frequent_questions.question: What should I do if I’m unsure whether a communication is permissible?
frequent_questions.answer: Direct the question to Legal or Compliance before communicating.
